mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.0%, consumer spending collapses and corporate earnings forecasts are downgraded, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 8.0%, or the signal loses predictive power after 2013.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and capital expenditure expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy by at least 15 basis points per month over a 24-month out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which triggers short-term equity momentum and positive 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during late-cycle expansions, with a negative Rank IC exceeding -0.05 in those periods.

mechanism_narrative: Steady monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding labor demand and rising household income, which supports corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across all tested rolling windows since 2005.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above a 0.4% monthly threshold forces expectations of tighter monetary policy, increasing discount rates and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in over 60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.4% threshold, invalidating the inflation-fear transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between stagnant quarterly GDP growth and rising unemployment indicates a deteriorating macroeconomic backdrop, triggering risk-off flows and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The divergence signal produces positive 60-day returns in more than half of historical occurrences, or fails to separate market regimes with a p-value below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly contraction in housing starts signals collapsing construction demand and negative wealth effects, triggering immediate risk aversion and negative 5-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines by more than 10% month-over-month
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns show no significant negative skew following HOUST declines exceeding 10%, with a t-statistic above -1.5 over the full sample.
